description: Visual identification rules for all 14 tattoo supply product categories. Each entry defines primary visual indicators and disambiguation rules for distinguishing similar products.

visual_indicators: cylindrical pen-like body 12-16cm
textured grip section (rubberized or knurled)
RCA connector or USB port at base
RCA clip cord cable attached
OLED/LED screen on body (wireless models)
battery indicator or voltage display
colored anodized aluminum body
button controls on body
cartridge attached at front with needle tip
metal tip with visible needle bar movement

distinguish_from.grip: machines have electronic components (screen, buttons, cable port). Grips are purely mechanical hollow tubes without electronics.
distinguish_from.wireless_battery: machines have visible needle bar/cartridge attachment at front. Batteries have contact pins at both ends and no moving parts.
distinguish_from.power_supply: machines are handheld pen-sized. Power supplies are desktop units or tube batteries with display.

photography_context: often shown with clip cord plugged in
may be disassembled into grip + body + battery sections
cartridge mounted at tip for context
commonly shot at 45-degree angle to show screen and grip

visual_indicators: small transparent/translucent cylinder 2-3cm
metal needle tip protruding from one end
colored O-ring at base (brand color coding system)
transparent membrane visible at bottom
arranged in tray with individual slots
metal tip with visible bevel/grind marks
color-coded housing by needle type or gauge
individual sterile packaging or bulk tray
visible needle count at tip (single vs multiple pins)

distinguish_from.needle_pack: cartridges have 3D plastic housing + O-ring + colored base. Needle packs are flat paper/plastic peel pouches containing bare needle bars.
distinguish_from.grip: cartridges are much smaller (2-3cm). Grips are 8-12cm hollow tubes for holding.
distinguish_from.machine_part: cartridges are disposable tips that attach to machine front. They are not part of the machine body.

photography_context: commonly displayed in tray configurations
often shot macro to show needle tip detail
brands emphasize color coding system in photos
frequently grouped by type (RL/RS/M1/CM) in rows

visual_indicators: glass or plastic bottle 15-120ml
colored liquid visible inside (black, white, colored pigments)
label with brand name and color/pigment name
dropper top, twist cap, or needle-tip dispenser
ink cap or ink cup nearby (small disposable cups)
multiple bottles arranged together as set
pigment settled at bottom (visible separation in some colors)
boxed set presentation (e.g. 6/12/24 color sets)

distinguish_from.aftercare_product: ink bottles contain colored liquid, labeled with pigment/color names. Aftercare products are opaque tubes or jars with cream, labeled 'healing' or 'ointment'.
distinguish_from.cleaning_supply: inks are labeled with color names and pigment codes. Cleaning products have disinfectant/sterilization labeling.
distinguish_from.accessory: ink bottles are liquid containers. Accessories are solid parts/tools.

photography_context: often shot in sets or color arrays
dropper detail frequently featured macro
ink caps with ink used as scale context
branding/label front-facing in commercial shots

visual_indicators: cylindrical tube shorter than machine
OLED display showing battery level percentage or voltage number
single button on body (power or +/- adjustment)
metal contact pins at one or both ends
charging port (USB-C) on side or bottom
anodized aluminum or matte black finish
brand logo prominently on body
LED indicator light (charging or power status)

distinguish_from.tattoo_machine: batteries have no cartridge holder, no needle bar mechanism. Purely a cylinder with contacts. No moving parts.
distinguish_from.grip: batteries have electronics (screen, button, charging port). Grips are passive mechanical tubes with no electronics.
distinguish_from.power_supply: batteries are compact and handheld. Power supplies are larger desktop units with multiple ports and wall power cord.

photography_context: often shown next to machine to demonstrate compatibility
OLED display frequently featured as premium signal
USB-C port detail highlighted in macro shots
commercial product lighting to emphasize anodized finish

visual_indicators: flat paper-plastic peel pouch with sterile seal
multiple pouches bundled together in pack
thin flat profile (contrast with cartridge 3D shape)
sterile indicator dot or stripe on pouch
label text: gauge-number-type format (e.g. 1003RL)
arranged in fan or row patterns for display
transparent window showing needle bar inside
peel-open edge visible
paper backing with printed specifications

distinguish_from.tattoo_cartridges: needle packs are flat peel pouches (single-use sterile wrap). Cartridges are rigid 3D plastic housings. If it stands up, it's a cartridge. If it lies flat, it's a needle pack.
distinguish_from.stencil_paper: needle packs are small individual pouches (~2x5cm). Stencil paper is larger flat sheets (~A4 size). Very different scale.
distinguish_from.glove: needle packs are paper/plastic pouches. Gloves are in boxes with visible glove cuffs.

photography_context: often displayed fan-spread to show label text
bundles shown with sterile seal intact
macro shot of label text for gauge/type legibility
brand emphasizes peel-open ease in demonstration

visual_indicators: hollow tube 8-12cm length
visible hollow interior channel (see-through when held up)
textured exterior (diamond knurling, groove pattern, or silicone sleeve)
aluminum, stainless steel, or titanium construction
O-rings at top and bottom ends
sold individually or in sets with different textures/colors
can have rubber/silicone cover sleeve over metal core
threaded ends for machine attachment

distinguish_from.tattoo_machine: grips are simple textured tubes with no electronics, no screen, no cable port, no needle mechanism.
distinguish_from.cartridge: grips are much larger (fits in full hand, 8-12cm). Cartridges are tiny (2-3cm).
distinguish_from.wireless_battery: grips have no electronics. No screen, no button, no charging port. Pure mechanical tube.

photography_context: often shown disassembled from machine
texture detail highlighted in macro
sets displayed in row to show color/texture variety
commonly shot on black background for metallic reflection

visual_indicators: desktop box form factor with display
digital voltage readout (LCD or LED digits)
control knob or +/- buttons
multiple output ports (RCA or binding posts)
RCA clip cord output cable connected
power cord connecting to wall outlet
foot pedal connected via cable
compact battery-style with screen (portable versions)

distinguish_from.wireless_battery: power supplies are larger desktop units with wall power cord and multiple ports. Not handheld. Not pen-shaped.
distinguish_from.machine: power supplies are supply units, not tattooing tools. No grip, no needle, no cartridge.
distinguish_from.accessory: power supplies are active electronic devices. Accessories are passive replacement parts.

photography_context: often shown on desk with cables visible
display screen featured as primary detail
foot pedal included in frame for context
workstation setup context (part of broader desk photo)

visual_indicators: tube, jar, pump bottle, or box container
opaque white or colored container (not transparent)
cream, balm, or lotion format (not liquid)
medical or clinical-style labeling
labeled as 'healing', 'ointment', 'balm', 'moisturizer', 'aftercare'
pump bottle or squeeze tube format
second skin / protective film in flat box or on backing paper
branding emphasizes natural or medical ingredients

distinguish_from.tattoo_ink: aftercare containers are opaque with cream/lotion textures. Ink bottles are transparent/translucent with visible colored liquid.
distinguish_from.cleaning_supply: aftercare is for POST-tattoo healing (skin application). Cleaning is for PRE-tattoo disinfection (surface/equipment).
distinguish_from.stencil_paper: aftercare is in tubes/jars/bottles. Stencil paper is flat paper sheets or rolls.

photography_context: often lifestyle photography rather than studio
tube squeezed with product coming out (demonstration)
natural soft lighting for 'gentle/healing' association
often alongside other studio items for context

visual_indicators: stack of paper sheets or roll format
purple, blue, or gold tinted surface (hectograph coating)
multi-layer construction (carbon paper-like)
packaged in reams, stacks, or rolls
characteristic hectograph purple color on transfer surface
cover sheet interleaving between coated sheets
purple residue on fingers or surfaces (tell-tale sign)
printed grid or design outlines possible on used sheets

distinguish_from.needle_pack: stencil paper is A4/A5 flat sheets. Needle packs are tiny individual pouches. Size difference is dramatic.
distinguish_from.paper_towel: stencil paper has smooth coated surface (not absorbent like towel) and distinct purple/blue color.
distinguish_from.packaging: stencil paper is the consumable itself, not its container.

photography_context: usually flat lay or stacked presentation
purple color is the key identifying feature
often shown with stencil printer (thermal) for context
corner of sheet lifted to show multi-layer construction

visual_indicators: box of disposable gloves
black or blue nitrile material (black = tattoo industry typical)
glove cuffs visible at box opening (pop-up dispensing)
textured fingertips visible on individual gloves
powder-free label on box front
examination glove-style packaging box
individual gloves partially pulled from box opening
size label (S/M/L/XL) on box front

distinguish_from.medical_supply: tattoo gloves are typically BLACK nitrile (industry standard for blood-borne pathogen protection). Medical gloves are blue or white.
distinguish_from.packaging: a glove box with gloves visible is a glove product. Empty box would be packaging.
distinguish_from.cleaning_supply: glove boxes contain the gloves themselves. Cleaning supplies are sprays, solutions, or sterilization items.

photography_context: box front label featured prominently
single glove pulled out showing texture
glove on hand with machine (workplace context)
often lifestyle context (held, worn) rather than pure product shot

visual_indicators: spray bottle format (most common)
green soap (characteristic translucent green color in clear bottle)
disinfectant labeling ('kills 99.9%', 'hospital grade')
sterilization pouches (paper + plastic front)
autoclave indicator tape (striped pattern that changes color)
barrier film rolls (plastic wrap for equipment)
sterilization wrap sheets (blue or white medical wrap)
dispenser bottles or wall-mounted dispenser units

distinguish_from.aftercare_product: cleaning is PRE-tattoo surface/equipment/tool disinfection. Aftercare is POST-tattoo skin healing.
distinguish_from.tattoo_ink: cleaning supplies are clear or green soaps, sprays, not pigmented like ink. Labels focus on sterilization not color.
distinguish_from.glove: cleaning supplies are liquids/sprays/wipes. Gloves are wearable disposables in boxes.

photography_context: often grouped as 'studio care' bundle
spray bottle is the most recognizable format
green soap in clear bottle is a tattoo industry icon
clinical clean lighting to reinforce sterile positioning

visual_indicators: small part or replacement component
cables and clip cords (RCA cable with connectors)
O-rings and gaskets (tiny rubber rings)
membranes (small transparent discs for cartridge base)
screws, wrenches, and small tools
tips and nozzles (replacement machine parts)
connectors and adapters (RCA, USB, threaded)
mouthpieces (for coil machines)
spare parts organized in small bags or boxes

distinguish_from.machine_part: accessories are replacement/spare parts for maintenance. Machine implies the complete functional tool.
distinguish_from.bundle_kit: accessories are individual spare parts. A bundle/kit is multiple items sold together as a package.
distinguish_from.packaging: accessories are the parts themselves. Packaging is the container/display.

photography_context: often macro detail shots
organized display (small parts in rows)
shown alongside machine or cartridge for scale context
functional context (tool with screw, cable plugged in)

visual_indicators: multiple different product types together in one frame
kit packaging or box containing varied items
starter kit: machine + cartridges + ink + accessories grouped
matching aesthetic or brand color across items
value bundle presentation (items in case or tray)
labeled as 'starter kit', 'bundle', 'combo', 'complete set'
items arranged aesthetically as a set (not individual sales)

distinguish_from.individual_product: kits contain 3+ different product types grouped together. Single product frame would show only one type of item.
distinguish_from.packaging: bundle/kit focuses on the CONTENTS (what's included). Packaging focuses on the CONTAINER (box, display case).

photography_context: flat lay or spread layout to show all items
items arranged in branded case or tray
lifestyle photography staging
each item individually visible for 'what's included' clarity

visual_indicators: box, case, or display container as main visual subject
foam insert with cutouts shaped for specific products
retail-ready packaging with brand graphics and logo
product clearly visible inside packaging (window or transparent)
exhibition or demo display stand
multiple items in organized case arrangement
retail shelf display unit with hanging tab
brand design/graphics dominate the visual

distinguish_from.bundle_kit: packaging emphasizes the container/display itself. Bundle/kit emphasizes the contents being sold together.
distinguish_from.accessory: packaging is the outer container. Accessories are the inner contents/parts.
distinguish_from.glove: a box with gloves visible inside would be 'glove' category. Packaging category is about the display/container as product.

photography_context: commercial product photography style most common
brand design and logo front and center
white or gradient background typical
box angled to show front and side panels
